recentConversations.text: Sync Evidence rebuilds the flat RAG house from the configured local source repos. It should answer who/what by listing sources and records, not by dropping you into raw notes.
Summarize the safest zero-cash revenue lane in one sentence.

I am on the local-rag path. I can help with the dashboard, mining lane choices, repo evidence, wallet receipts, cloud mirrors, or local fleet controls. Ask in plain language and I will route it to the safest visible lane.
Summarize the safest zero-cash revenue lane in one sentence.

"The safest zero-cash revenue lane for Lantern OS within a local-first operator dashboard is to generate income through advertising and service fees, while ensuring user privacy by implementing decentralized data storage and robust encryption."

check mcp tools status

To check the status of Multi-Chain Protocol (MCP) tools within the Lantern Operator Dashboard, you can follow these steps:

1. Log in to your operator dashboard.
2. Navigate to the MCP section. Depending on the specific interface design, this could be located under Network, Chains, or Multi-Chain Management.
3. Once you're in the MCP section, you should find an overview of installed tools and their current status (e.g., active, deactivated).
4. If any issues are detected, error messages or alerts will be displayed to guide troubleshooting and resolution processes.

If you encounter difficulties locating the MCP tools status, consult the user manual or support resources provided by your operator dashboard vendor for specific guidance.
check mcp tools status
